classifications.label: GHOST
classifications.note: CLI is a thin Typer app whose lint subcommand takes no provider option. No shared provider state across subcommands. Original mental model was wrong.

classifications.label: REAL
classifications.note: _CITATION_RE matches paths with backslashes. Path resolution then normalizes both separators in pathlib, which can mask cross-platform bugs.
classifications.remediation: Document a 'use forward slashes' guideline in the antemortem template; reject backslash paths in lint with a clear error.

classifications.label: GHOST
classifications.note: DECISION_LABELS is a single source of truth shared by schema and rule code. No drift possible.

classifications.label: UNRESOLVED
classifications.note: Recon scope did not include profiling work. Worth measuring on a large repo before claiming GHOST or REAL.

new_traps.hypothesis: AntemortemOutput.decision is Optional; a missing field could silently produce a wrong verdict.
new_traps.label: NEW
new_traps.note: The decision field is `Literal[...] | None`. CLI must explicitly compute the decision after classification rather than trusting the model's payload.
new_traps.remediation: Always run compute_decision() in the CLI; never read decision directly from the LLM payload.

spec_mutations: Document a 'forward slashes only' rule for citation paths in the antemortem template.
Add a check in the demo recorder to reject backslash citation paths.
Note citation-count perf as a 'When NOT to use' caveat in the README.
decision_rationale: One high-severity REAL finding (citation regex) has a concrete remediation; one new finding has a clear mitigation. No unmitigated high-severity findings — proceed with the spec_mutations above applied.
